claim_boundary: The ledger proves only current registered behavior/source/owner/path accounting; sibling evidence and final release authority remain separate.
do_not_use_when: The scope is helper internals, no external behavior inventory exists, or a sibling evidence route owns the question.

downstream_targets.condition: Commitment/DCAR/PPA finite coverage must be generated.
Commitments must bind model, owner code, and tests.
Commitment shards need child-suite ownership and freshness.

native_checks.evidence_source: current model runner receipt and final report
native_checks.summary: Run the behavior commitment ledger model and failure variants.

route_summary: Bind external behavior commitments to fresh sources, exactly one primary owner model, dependencies, evidence, and path authority.

source_requirements.summary: Every current source maps to commitments and every commitment maps back to fresh source evidence.
Each commitment has exactly one primary owner and path-sensitive rows consume internal Primary Path Authority evidence.
Missing DCAR, TestMesh, alignment, risk, replacement, dependency, or PPA evidence blocks broad confidence.

use_when: External behavior must be added, changed, removed, backfilled, miss-checked, or fully accounted before a broad claim.

workflow.summary: Define the work-package boundary and select one of the six ledger modes.
Inventory fresh source surfaces and external commitments bidirectionally with one primary owner each.
Record dependencies, replacement/model-sync state, evidence, and internal PPA bindings for path-sensitive rows.
Run ledger and Primary Path Authority checks plus required DCAR/shard/risk projections.
Block broad confidence on any missing, extra, overlap, stale, dependency, disposition, or PPA gap.
